Weak Free Choice (2025) (wersja instrukcji: soft; z przypomnieniami)

Bartosz

library(readr)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggpubr)
library(flextable)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(sjPlot)
library(emmeans)
data_raw <- read.csv("results.csv")
#table(data_raw$PARTICIPANT) 
#table(data_raw$ITEM1_TYPE, useNA = "ifany")
#table(data_raw$ITEM2_TYPE, useNA = "ifany")
data <- data_raw %>% 
  mutate(
    resp = case_when(
      response %in% c("b", "B") ~ 1,
      response %in% c("n", "N") ~ 0,
  ),
    trial_type = case_when(
      (ITEM1_TYPE == "accepts" & ITEM2_TYPE == "wants")  ~ "W + A",
      (ITEM1_TYPE == "wants" & ITEM2_TYPE == "accepts")  ~ "W + A",
      (ITEM1_TYPE == "accepts" & ITEM2_TYPE == "dislikes")  ~ "D + A",
      (ITEM1_TYPE == "dislikes" & ITEM2_TYPE == "accepts")  ~ "D + A",
      (ITEM1_TYPE == "wants" & ITEM2_TYPE == "dislikes")  ~ "W + D",
      (ITEM1_TYPE == "dislikes" & ITEM2_TYPE == "wants")  ~ "W + D",
      
      (ITEM1_TYPE == "wants" & ITEM2_TYPE == "")  ~ "W",
      (ITEM1_TYPE == "dislikes" & ITEM2_TYPE == "")  ~ "D",
      (ITEM1_TYPE == "accepts" & ITEM2_TYPE == "")  ~ "A",
      
      (ITEM1_TYPE == "wants" & ITEM2_TYPE == "wants")  ~ "W + W",
      (ITEM1_TYPE == "dislikes" & ITEM2_TYPE == "dislikes")  ~ "D + D",
      (ITEM1_TYPE == "accepts" & ITEM2_TYPE == "accepts")  ~ "A + A",
    ),
  condition = case_when(
    condition %in% c("DOUBLE_CONTROL", "DOUBLE_CONTROL_MIND") ~ "DOUBLE_CONTROL",
    condition %in% c("SINGLE_CONTROL", "SINGLE_CONTROL_MIND") ~ "SINGLE_CONTROL",
    condition %in% c("EXPERIMENTAL", "EXPERIMENTAL_MIND") ~ "EXPERIMENTAL",
  )
  
  ) %>% 
  filter(condition %in% c("DOUBLE_CONTROL", "SINGLE_CONTROL", "EXPERIMENTAL",
                          "SINGLE_CONTROL_MIND", "DOUBLE_CONTROL_MIND", "EXPERIMENTAL_MIND")) %>% 
  filter(SESSION == "EXPERIMENTAL")

Answers

stats <- data %>% 
  group_by(PARTICIPANT) %>% 
  group_by(condition, verb, trial_type, correct) %>% 
  summarise(M = mean(resp),
            SD = sd(resp),
            n = n()
            )
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'condition', 'verb', 'trial_type'. You can
## override using the `.groups` argument.
data_collapsed <- data %>% 
  group_by(PARTICIPANT, condition, verb, trial_type, correct) %>% 
  summarise(RESPONSE = mean(resp))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'PARTICIPANT', 'condition', 'verb',
## 'trial_type'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
ggbarplot(data = data_collapsed,
          x = "correct",
          y = "RESPONSE",
          fill = "trial_type",
          facet.by = c("condition", "verb"),
          add = "mean_se",
          position = position_dodge(0.9)
          )

stats %>% flextable() %>% colformat_double(digits = 2) %>% autofit()

Odpowiedzi przekodowane na 1 = Correct, 0 = Incorrect (oprócz experimental)

data$condition <- factor(data$condition, levels = c("DOUBLE_CONTROL", "SINGLE_CONTROL", "EXPERIMENTAL"))
data <- data %>% mutate(
  resp_adj = case_when(
    (resp == 1 & correct == "Y" ) ~ 1,
    (resp == 0 & correct == "Y" ) ~ 0,
    (resp == 1 & correct == "N" ) ~ 0,
    (resp == 0 & correct == "N" ) ~ 1,
    (resp == 1 & correct == "Y/N" ) ~ 1,
    (resp == 0 & correct == "Y/N" ) ~ 0,
  )
)
data_collapsed <- data %>% 
  filter(verb == "want") %>% 
  group_by(PARTICIPANT, condition, correct, trial_type) %>% 
  summarise(RESP_adj = mean(resp_adj))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'PARTICIPANT', 'condition', 'correct'. You
## can override using the `.groups` argument.
ggbarplot(data = data_collapsed,
          x = "correct",
          y = "RESP_adj",
          fill = "trial_type",
          add = "mean_se",
          facet.by = "condition",
          position = position_dodge(0.9)
          )

ggbarplot(data = data %>% filter(verb == "want"),
          x = "correct",
          y = "resp_adj",
          fill = "trial_type",
          add = "mean_se",
          position = position_dodge(0.9)
          )

Full model

data <- data %>% mutate(
  single_or_double = case_when(
    condition == "SINGLE_CONTROL" ~ "SINGLE",
    .default = "DOUBLE"
  )
)
fit <- glmer(resp_adj ~ trial_type * single_or_double + (1|PARTICIPANT), 
             data = data %>% filter(verb == "want"),
             control = glmerControl(optimizer = "Nelder_Mead", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 10e5)),
             family = binomial)
## fixed-effect model matrix is rank deficient so dropping 3 columns / coefficients
tab_model(fit)
  resp_adj
Predictors Odds Ratios CI p
(Intercept) 1.79 1.41 – 2.27 <0.001
trial type [D] 3.22 2.42 – 4.28 <0.001
trial type D + A 0.15 0.12 – 0.20 <0.001
trial type [W] 3.22 2.42 – 4.28 <0.001
trial type W + A 0.72 0.56 – 0.91 0.008
trial type W + D 0.29 0.23 – 0.38 <0.001
single or double [SINGLE] 1.05 0.83 – 1.32 0.696
trial type [D] × single
or double [SINGLE] 		0.78 0.54 – 1.13 0.184
trial type [W] × single
or double [SINGLE] 		1.02 0.70 – 1.49 0.911
Random Effects
σ2 3.29
τ00 PARTICIPANT 0.39
ICC 0.11
N PARTICIPANT 58
Observations 5916
Marginal R2 / Conditional R2 0.226 / 0.308

Model for EXPERIMENTAL trials

fit <- glmer(resp_adj ~ trial_type + (trial_type|PARTICIPANT), 
             data = data %>% filter(condition == "EXPERIMENTAL" & verb == "want"),
             family = binomial)
tab_model(fit)
  resp_adj
Predictors Odds Ratios CI p
(Intercept) 0.10 0.04 – 0.22 <0.001
trial type W + A 12.22 5.18 – 28.81 <0.001
trial type W + D 3.75 1.66 – 8.47 0.001
Random Effects
σ2 3.29
τ00 PARTICIPANT 6.08
τ11 PARTICIPANT.trial_typeW + A 5.39
τ11 PARTICIPANT.trial_typeW + D 4.33
ρ01 -0.52
-0.68
ICC 0.60
N PARTICIPANT 58
Observations 1740
Marginal R2 / Conditional R2 0.112 / 0.648

Participants’ level distribution of responses

want_exp <- data %>%                       # 1) keep only EXPERIMENTAL ‘want’ trials
  filter(condition == "EXPERIMENTAL",
         verb       == "want")

prop_by_part <- want_exp %>%               # 2) participant-level response rate
  group_by(PARTICIPANT, trial_type) %>% 
  summarise(p = mean(resp_adj), n = n(), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(prop_by_part, aes(p)) +             # 3) visualise
  geom_histogram(bins = 10, colour = "grey30") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(.10, .90), linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  facet_grid(. ~ trial_type) +
  labs(x = "Participant mean (resp_adj)", 
       y = "Number of participants",
       title = "Distribution of individual response rates – EXPERIMENTAL condition")

library(diptest)

Test for bimodal distribution

D + A

dip.test(prop_by_part %>% filter(trial_type == "D + A") %>% pull(p))
## 
##  Hartigans' dip test for unimodality / multimodality
## 
## data:  prop_by_part %>% filter(trial_type == "D + A") %>% pull(p)
## D = 0.068966, p-value = 0.03292
## alternative hypothesis: non-unimodal, i.e., at least bimodal

W + A

dip.test(prop_by_part %>% filter(trial_type == "W + A") %>% pull(p))
## 
##  Hartigans' dip test for unimodality / multimodality
## 
## data:  prop_by_part %>% filter(trial_type == "W + A") %>% pull(p)
## D = 0.10345, p-value = 2.439e-05
## alternative hypothesis: non-unimodal, i.e., at least bimodal

W + D

dip.test(prop_by_part %>% filter(trial_type == "W + D") %>% pull(p))
## 
##  Hartigans' dip test for unimodality / multimodality
## 
## data:  prop_by_part %>% filter(trial_type == "W + D") %>% pull(p)
## D = 0.060345, p-value = 0.1033
## alternative hypothesis: non-unimodal, i.e., at least bimodal

Response times

# Filtering for outliers
lower_ms   <- 200
mad_cutoff <- 2.5 # Metoda Kontka
#mad_cutoff <- 2
filter_rt_outliers <- function(data, rt_col = "rt") {
  data %>% 
    filter(.data[[rt_col]] >= lower_ms) %>%                     
    mutate(log_rt = log(.data[[rt_col]])) %>%                   
    mutate(
      med_log = median(log_rt,          na.rm = TRUE),
      mad_log = mad   (log_rt, constant = 1, na.rm = TRUE)      
    ) %>% 
    filter(abs(log_rt - med_log) <= mad_cutoff * mad_log) %>%   
    select(-log_rt, -med_log, -mad_log)
}

data_clean <- filter_rt_outliers(data, "rt")

Raw RTs

data_clean$resp_nom <- ifelse(data_clean$resp == 1, "TRUE", "FALSE")

stats <- data_clean %>% 
  group_by(PARTICIPANT) %>% 
  group_by(resp_nom, condition, verb, trial_type, correct) %>% 
  summarise(M = mean(rt),
            SD = sd(rt),
            n = n()
            )
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'resp_nom', 'condition', 'verb',
## 'trial_type'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
data_collapsed <- data_clean %>% 
  group_by(PARTICIPANT, resp_nom, condition, verb, trial_type, correct) %>% 
  summarise(RT = mean(rt))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'PARTICIPANT', 'resp_nom', 'condition',
## 'verb', 'trial_type'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
ggline(data = data_collapsed %>% filter(verb == "want"),
          x = "resp_nom",
          y = "RT",
          color = "trial_type",
          facet.by = c("condition", "correct"),
          add = "mean_se",
          position = position_dodge(0.9)
          )

stats %>% flextable() %>% colformat_double(digits = 2) %>% autofit()

data_clean$condition <- factor(data_clean$condition, levels = c("DOUBLE_CONTROL", "SINGLE_CONTROL", "EXPERIMENTAL"))
data_clean <- data_clean %>% mutate(
  resp_nom_adj = case_when(
    (resp_nom == "TRUE" & correct == "Y" ) ~ "TRUE",
    (resp_nom == "FALSE" & correct == "Y" ) ~ "FALSE",
    (resp_nom == "TRUE" & correct == "N") ~ "FALSE",
    (resp_nom == "FALSE" & correct == "N" ) ~ "TRUE",
    (resp_nom == "TRUE" & correct == "Y/N" ) ~ "TRUE",
    (resp_nom == "FALSE" & correct == "Y/N" ) ~ "FALSE",
  )
)

Full model

data_collapsed <- data_clean %>% 
  filter(verb == "want") %>% 
  group_by(PARTICIPANT, trial_type, resp_nom_adj) %>% 
  summarise(RT = mean(rt))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'PARTICIPANT', 'trial_type'. You can
## override using the `.groups` argument.
ggline(data = data_collapsed,
          x = "resp_nom_adj",
          y = "RT",
          color = "trial_type",
          add = "mean_se",
          position = position_dodge(0.9)
          )

ggline(data = data_clean %>% filter(verb == "want"),
          x = "resp_nom_adj",
          y = "rt",
          color = "trial_type",
          add = "mean_se",
          position = position_dodge(0.9)
          )

data_collapsed <- data_clean %>% 
  filter(verb == "want") %>% 
  group_by(PARTICIPANT, condition, trial_type, resp_nom_adj) %>% 
  summarise(RT = mean(rt))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'PARTICIPANT', 'condition', 'trial_type'.
## You can override using the `.groups` argument.
ggline(data = data_collapsed,
          x = "resp_nom_adj",
          y = "RT",
          color = "condition",
          add = "mean_se",
          facet.by = "trial_type",
          position = position_dodge(0.9)
          )

ggline(data = data_clean %>% filter(verb == "want"),
          x = "resp_nom_adj",
          y = "rt",
          color = "condition",
          add = "mean_se",
          facet.by = "trial_type",
          position = position_dodge(0.9)
          )

fit <- lmer(rt ~ resp_nom_adj * trial_type + (1|PARTICIPANT) + (1|item), 
             data = data_clean %>% filter(verb == "want"))

tab_model(fit)

Model for EXPERIMENTAL trials

data_collapsed <- data_clean %>% 
  filter(verb == "want", condition == "EXPERIMENTAL") %>% 
  group_by(PARTICIPANT, trial_type, resp_nom_adj) %>% 
  summarise(RT = mean(rt))

          x = "resp_nom_adj",
          y = "RT",
          color = "trial_type",
          add = "mean_se",
          position = position_dodge(0.9)
          )

                                    condition == "EXPERIMENTAL"),
          x = "resp_nom_adj",
          y = "rt",
          color = "trial_type",
          add = "mean_se",
          position = position_dodge(0.9)
          )

Log-transformed

data_clean$rt_log <- log(data_clean$rt)
data_clean$resp_nom <- ifelse(data_clean$resp == 1, "TRUE", "FALSE")

stats <- data_clean %>% 
  group_by(PARTICIPANT) %>% 
  group_by(resp_nom, condition, verb, trial_type, correct) %>% 
  summarise(M = mean(rt_log),
            SD = sd(rt_log),
            n = n()
            )
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'resp_nom', 'condition', 'verb',
## 'trial_type'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
data_collapsed <- data_clean %>% 
  group_by(PARTICIPANT, resp_nom, condition, verb, trial_type, correct) %>% 
  summarise(RT_log = mean(rt_log))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'PARTICIPANT', 'resp_nom', 'condition',
## 'verb', 'trial_type'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
ggline(data = data_collapsed %>% filter(verb == "want"),
          x = "resp_nom",
          y = "RT_log",
          color = "trial_type",
          facet.by = c("condition", "correct"),
          add = "mean_se",
          position = position_dodge(0.9)
          )

stats %>% flextable() %>% colformat_double(digits = 2) %>% autofit()

data_clean$condition <- factor(data_clean$condition, levels = c("DOUBLE_CONTROL", "SINGLE_CONTROL", "EXPERIMENTAL"))
data_clean <- data_clean %>% mutate(
  resp_nom_adj = case_when(
    (resp_nom == "TRUE" & correct == "Y" ) ~ "TRUE",
    (resp_nom == "FALSE" & correct == "Y" ) ~ "FALSE",
    (resp_nom == "TRUE" & correct == "N") ~ "FALSE",
    (resp_nom == "FALSE" & correct == "N" ) ~ "TRUE",
    (resp_nom == "TRUE" & correct == "Y/N" ) ~ "TRUE",
    (resp_nom == "FALSE" & correct == "Y/N" ) ~ "FALSE",
  )
)

Full model

data_collapsed <- data_clean %>% 
  filter(verb == "want") %>% 
  group_by(PARTICIPANT, trial_type, resp_nom_adj) %>% 
  summarise(RT_log = mean(rt_log))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'PARTICIPANT', 'trial_type'. You can
## override using the `.groups` argument.
ggline(data = data_collapsed,
          x = "resp_nom_adj",
          y = "RT_log",
          color = "trial_type",
          add = "mean_se",
          position = position_dodge(0.9)
          )

ggline(data = data_clean %>% filter(verb == "want"),
          x = "resp_nom_adj",
          y = "rt_log",
          color = "trial_type",
          add = "mean_se",
          position = position_dodge(0.9)
          )

data_collapsed <- data_clean %>% 
  filter(verb == "want") %>% 
  group_by(PARTICIPANT, condition, trial_type, resp_nom_adj) %>% 
  summarise(RT_log = mean(rt_log))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'PARTICIPANT', 'condition', 'trial_type'.
## You can override using the `.groups` argument.
ggline(data = data_collapsed,
          x = "resp_nom_adj",
          y = "RT_log",
          color = "condition",
          add = "mean_se",
          facet.by = "trial_type",
          position = position_dodge(0.9)
          )

ggline(data = data_clean %>% filter(verb == "want"),
          x = "resp_nom_adj",
          y = "rt_log",
          color = "condition",
          add = "mean_se",
          facet.by = "trial_type",
          position = position_dodge(0.9)
          )

fit <- lmer(rt_log ~ resp_nom_adj * trial_type + (1|PARTICIPANT) + (1|item), 
             data = data_clean %>% filter(verb == "want"))

tab_model(fit)
  rt_log
Predictors Estimates CI p
(Intercept) 7.26 7.19 – 7.34 <0.001
resp nom adj [TRUE] 0.12 0.06 – 0.17 <0.001
trial type [D] 0.28 0.20 – 0.35 <0.001
trial type D + A 0.43 0.36 – 0.50 <0.001
trial type [W] 0.26 0.18 – 0.33 <0.001
trial type W + A 0.38 0.30 – 0.46 <0.001
trial type W + D 0.40 0.33 – 0.47 <0.001
resp nom adj [TRUE] ×
trial type [D] 		-0.19 -0.27 – -0.11 <0.001
resp nom adj [TRUE] ×
trial type D + A 		-0.31 -0.42 – -0.21 <0.001
resp nom adj [TRUE] ×
trial type [W] 		-0.18 -0.26 – -0.10 <0.001
resp nom adj [TRUE] ×
trial type W + A 		-0.14 -0.23 – -0.05 0.002
resp nom adj [TRUE] ×
trial type W + D 		-0.13 -0.22 – -0.03 0.008
Random Effects
σ2 0.15
τ00 item 0.00
τ00 PARTICIPANT 0.06
ICC 0.27
N PARTICIPANT 58
N item 102
Observations 5194
Marginal R2 / Conditional R2 0.063 / 0.320 
emm_resp_by_cond <- emmeans(fit, ~ resp_nom_adj | trial_type, lmer.df = "asymptotic")
broom::tidy(pairs(emm_resp_by_cond, adjust = "tukey")) %>% flextable() %>% colformat_double(digits = 3) %>% autofit()

Model for EXPERIMENTAL trials

data_collapsed <- data_clean %>% 
  filter(verb == "want", condition == "EXPERIMENTAL") %>% 
  group_by(PARTICIPANT, trial_type, resp_nom_adj) %>% 
  summarise(RT_log = mean(rt_log))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'PARTICIPANT', 'trial_type'. You can
## override using the `.groups` argument.
ggline(data = data_collapsed,
          x = "resp_nom_adj",
          y = "RT_log",
          color = "trial_type",
          add = "mean_se",
          position = position_dodge(0.9)
          )

ggline(data = data_clean %>% filter(verb == "want",
                                    condition == "EXPERIMENTAL"),
          x = "resp_nom_adj",
          y = "rt_log",
          color = "trial_type",
          add = "mean_se",
          position = position_dodge(0.9)
          )

fit <- lmer(rt_log ~ trial_type * resp_nom + (1|PARTICIPANT) + (1|item) ,
             data = data_clean %>% filter(verb == "want", condition == "EXPERIMENTAL"))

tab_model(fit)
  rt_log
Predictors Estimates CI p
(Intercept) 7.68 7.60 – 7.75 <0.001
trial type W + A -0.03 -0.09 – 0.04 0.389
